ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 represents a generic chicken sandwich. The Jollibee Original variant is a specific commercial product not individually coded in FoodOn; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers chicken burger/sandwich as a category. The specific Jollibee Original formulation is not individually coded; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 represents chicken sandwich generically. The specific commercial variant is not individually coded; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One chicken sandwich weighing approximately 160 g, consisting of a breaded fried chicken fillet served in a soft bun with sauce and lettuce.

nutrient_source.title: Fast Foods, Chicken Sandwich, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21120)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for a plain fast-food chicken sandwich (NDB 21120 / FDC 175194) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent a breaded fried chicken fillet in a plain bun, which is the closest approved-source match to the Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich. Sodium may be slightly lower than the Jollibee product; values are adapted from USDA data and noted accordingly. Per-100g values derived from USDA reported per-sandwich values divided by reported weight.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fast-food chicken sandwich / burger bun composite — University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate based on white bread bun (GI ~70) and fried chicken fillet (GI ~40) composite
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich. GI estimated at 66 using a weighted composite of the soft white burger bun (GI ~70, University of Sydney GI Database, white bread reference) and the breaded fried chicken fillet (GI ~40, low-GI protein-fat component). Carbohydrate contribution from bun dominates; composite GI weighted by carbohydrate content per component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 42.4) / 100 ≈ 28.0, rounded to 28.2 using 160 g serving with 26.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~530 mg per 100 g; ~848 mg per sandwich)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this sandwich as a single serving provides approximately 37% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and regular intake may contribute to elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic load (~28 per serving) and refined carbohydrate from white bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this sandwich with caution as the high glycemic load (~28 per serving) from the refined white bun can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables are advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this sandwich contributes saturated fat (~2.4 g/100 g), trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), and high sodium (~530 mg/100 g), all of which may adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure; occasional consumption within a heart-healthy dietary pattern is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich is a fast-food chicken sandwich served at Jollibee restaurants in Singapore, including the Bugis Village outlet. It consists of a deep-fried, breaded chicken breast fillet served in a soft white burger bun, typically accompanied by mayonnaise-based sauce and lettuce. The chicken fillet is marinated and coated in a seasoned wheat flour breading before being deep-fried, resulting in a crispy exterior and juicy interior. The sandwich is certified Halal and is a popular item in the Filipino-origin fast-food chain's Singapore menu. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-to-high calorie item (~400 kcal per sandwich) with significant protein (~22 g per sandwich), moderate fat (~17 g), and substantial refined carbohydrate from the bun (~42 g). Sodium content is high (~848 mg per sandwich), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load is high (~28 per serving) due to the refined white bun, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and sodium content also make it a moderate concern for cardiovascular health. It is best consumed occasionally as part of a balanced diet. The sandwich is comparable in nutritional profile to other fast-food chicken sandwiches such as those from McDonald's or KFC Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Jollibee chicken sandwich?
Is the Jollibee chicken sandwich halal?
Is chicken sandwich good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the USDA SR Legacy entry for a generic plain fast-food chicken sandwich (FDC 175194), which is the closest approved-source match. The Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich is a specific commercial product whose exact formulation is not published in an approved primary nutrient database. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference values for white bread and fried chicken components. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC 175194, Fast Foods, Chicken Sandwich, plain) as no HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific product. Adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. 5. GI is an analogous estimate (GI=66, medium) based on University of Sydney GI Database reference values for white burger bun and fried chicken fillet components; no direct GI measurement for the Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich exists in an approved source. 6. The food is tagged Halal per Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification as indicated in the field survey cuisine tags. 7. Multilingual names are provided for English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for fast-food items served in Singapore. 8. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact ontology code exists for this specific commercial product.

improvement_actions_taken: Derived per-100g nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 175194 (Fast Foods, Chicken Sandwich, plain) as no primary Singapore or regional source covers this specific product.
Added multilingual names for all five Singapore-required languages (Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Constructed analogous GI estimate (GI=66, medium) from University of Sydney GI Database white bread and fried chicken reference values.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Used closest_parent match type for all three ontology systems with gap notes.
Populated all three health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
